target.name: Steady Energy
target.founder: Tommi Nyman, Hannes Haapalahti, Petteri Tenhunen

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $1.8B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Steady Energy is a Series G climate and energy technology company founded by Tommi Nyman, Hannes Haapalahti, and Petteri Tenhunen. The company has raised €32M to date. The Climate & Energy sector contains 9 peer companies, including 1 public comparable (LNZA) and 8 private peers such as Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or valuation-tier aligned comparables to support a reliable median-based approach.

Valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 1.25x step-up multiplier typical of Series G advancement. Using €32M in total raised as a valuation proxy yields a base value of $1.8B, which steps up to $2.2B when accounting for Series G maturity and expected progression. This method is grounded in observed capital-raising patterns within frontier energy and climate technology but carries inherent limitations absent direct peer benchmarking.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.9B to $2.5B, with a median point of $2.2B. This range reflects a single methodological input and should be interpreted with caution. Key uncertainties include (1) the absence of granular Stage G peer data in the narrow Climate & Energy subsector, (2) the appropriateness of the 1.25x multiplier to Steady Energy's specific unit economics and market position, and (3) macro conditions affecting late-stage energy tech valuations. Additional revenue, margin, or disclosed fundraising data would materially strengthen conviction in any point estimate within this range.
